explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: Policy documents detail data use for business purposes such as analytics and risk management. Additionally, the Terms of Service prohibit the use of company content for developing or training AI, machine learning, or large language models without a separate agreement.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This company-owned privacy policy document provides evidence for the governance, privacy, and transparency pillars of responsible AI. It supports governance by detailing data use for business purposes like analytics and risk management, and privacy by outlining opt-out mechanisms for targeted advertising and data sharing, as well as mentioning data retention for AI training. Transparency is supported by the policy's mention of using AI tools and a commitment to inform users before recording.
relevant_sources.title: Global Privacy Policy

relevant_sources.summary: The "Terms of Service" document provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. This is because the terms explicitly prohibit the use of company content for developing or training AI, machine learning, or large language models without a separate agreement, demonstrating a clear policy and control over data usage for external AI development.
relevant_sources.title: Terms of Service

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.findings: The company's privacy policy outlines opt-out mechanisms for targeted advertising and data sharing. It also mentions data retention practices specifically for AI training purposes.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

relevant_sources.summary: This company-owned privacy policy document provides evidence for the governance, privacy, and transparency pillars of responsible AI. It supports governance by detailing data use for business purposes like analytics and risk management, and privacy by outlining opt-out mechanisms for targeted advertising and data sharing, as well as mentioning data retention for AI training. Transparency is supported by the policy's mention of using AI tools and a commitment to inform users before recording.
relevant_sources.title: Global Privacy Policy

transparency.findings: The company's policy documents mention the use of AI tools. These documents also state a commitment to inform users before any recording takes place.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: This company-owned privacy policy document provides evidence for the governance, privacy, and transparency pillars of responsible AI. It supports governance by detailing data use for business purposes like analytics and risk management, and privacy by outlining opt-out mechanisms for targeted advertising and data sharing, as well as mentioning data retention for AI training. Transparency is supported by the policy's mention of using AI tools and a commitment to inform users before recording.
relevant_sources.title: Global Privacy Policy

summary.overall_findings: A-Mark Precious Metals' policy documents outline opt-out mechanisms for targeted advertising and data sharing, addressing privacy. These disclosures address 3 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars at the policy level, with transparency materials mentioning the use of AI tools and a commitment to inform users before recording. Governance policies further detail data use for business purposes, including analytics and risk management. Based on 3 publicly available sources, no qualifying public evidence was found for fairness, explainability, and 2 additional pillars.
